summary: nock-helper postinstall exfiltrates npm/AWS/GitHub tokens and crypto wallet data to hardcoded C2
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js harvests installer-side secrets and exfiltrates them to the hardcoded C2 at http://149.28.127.35:8888 over plain HTTP. The script reads ~/.npmrc, ~/.env, and ~/.git-credentials and regex-extracts npm tokens (`npm_[A-Za-z0-9]{36}`), AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID/AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, and GITHUB_TOKEN values. It additionally walks Chrome/Brave/Edge/Chromium/Vivaldi/Opera profile directories against a hardcoded list of 71 crypto-wallet extension IDs (MetaMask `nkbihfbeogaeaoehlefnkodbefgpgknn`, Phantom, Coinbase Wallet, Trust, Ledger Live, Trezor Suite, etc.), reading Local Extension Settings LevelDB `.log` files and matching regexes for `vault`, `mnemonic`, `privateKey`, `keyring`, and encrypted material. A `scanSeedPhrases()` routine walks ~/Documents, ~/Desktop, and ~/Downloads matching file contents against BIP-39 seed word lists. All collected data is POSTed as JSON to the hardcoded C2. The package deceptively markets itself as a `nock` HTTP-mock / lodash utility helper; index.js is an empty stub that self-admits `The real payload is in postinstall.js`, and postinstall.js header reads `Token harvester + Crypto wallet scanner / Runs on npm install. Silent. Zero trace.`

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
